infobox.title: Fraud Detection System
infobox.imageCaption: Admin dashboard showing ML prediction analytics

lead:
The **Online Transaction Fraud Detection System** is the MCA Final Year Academic Project (3rd Semester), built as a full-stack banking fraud detection platform using **ASP.NET Web Forms** and a **4-algorithm ML ensemble**. It classifies every transaction through Logistic Regression, KNN, SVM, and a Convolutional Neural Network — if 3 or more algorithms vote that a transaction is fraudulent, it is automatically blocked.

The system includes real-time fraud detection, an intrusion detection module that tracks failed logins by IP and geolocation, SMTP email alerts, and a full admin panel with Google Charts analytics. The code is publicly released on GitHub.

content.content:
All four ML algorithms are implemented directly in C# rather than using a Python ML library. This was a deliberate constraint of the project — the entire stack had to be a single ASP.NET application, not a Python microservice.

The **ensemble decision rule** is simple and interpretable: majority voting. If 3 or more of the 4 algorithms independently classify a transaction as fraudulent, the transaction is blocked. This reduces false positives compared to single-algorithm approaches (where one uncertain model can trigger a block) while maintaining sensitivity.

rows.Notes: Binary fraud/no-fraud classification on 4 normalised features
K=3 nearest neighbours in feature space
Support Vector Machine — finds maximum-margin decision boundary
Convolutional Neural Network — the only deep learning model in the ensemble

content.title: Ensemble decision rule
content.content: 3 or more out of 4 algorithms vote fraud → transaction blocked. This majority-voting approach is more robust than any single algorithm and reduces both false positives and false negatives compared to relying on the CNN alone.

rows.Detail: Value of the transaction in rupees
Frequency of recent transactions from the account
0 = known safe location, 1 = flagged risky location
0 = business hours, 1 = odd hours (late night / early morning)

content.content:
**Fraud Detection:** Real-time ensemble ML voting on every transaction. Transaction blocked when 3+ algorithms vote fraud. The blocking is immediate — no human review queue.

**Intrusion Detection:** Failed logins are tracked with IP address, GPS geolocation, and timestamp. Auto account blocking triggers when 5+ failed transactions occur within 1 hour. SMTP email alerts are sent on suspicious activity.

**Admin Panel:** Full user management, bank CRUD operations, intrusion log viewer, transaction analytics with Google Charts, and ML prediction dashboard showing per-algorithm vote counts.

**User Side:** Supports creating bank accounts (SBI, HDFC, Karnataka Bank), adding wallet funds, transferring money with live fraud detection, viewing account statements, and a security key required per account for all transactions.

content.title: Security
content.content: Parameterized SQL queries throughout (SQL injection prevention). Session management with proper logout. Security key per bank account required for all transaction operations.

This project was the **3rd Semester final year academic submission** for the MCA programme at SIT Tumakuru. The 3rd semester is designated for the final year academic project; the 4th (final) semester is reserved for the industry internship.

The choice of C# and ASP.NET rather than Python and Flask was intentional — C# and .NET represent a professional enterprise stack, and implementing the ML algorithms from scratch in a statically-typed language demonstrates understanding of the underlying mathematics rather than just calling a Python library.

For contrast with the CHIAC ASI internship ML work (Python, QLoRA, transformer models), see [[thoshan-flash|Thoshan Flash]].
